channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: White House OPM orders all DEI offices to begin closing by end of day Wednesday
item.description: The Office of Personnel Management tells agency and department heads they must close all DEIA offices by the end of Wednesday and put government workers in those offices on paid leave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration throws out policies limiting migrant arrests at sensitive spots like churches
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen as the new president seeks to make good on campaign promises to carry
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth's former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senators vetting the nomination of Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Key career officials at Justice Department reassigned to different positions, AP sources say
item.description: The Justice Department has reassigned key senior officials across multiple divisions as part of a leadership shakeup ahead of the expected confirmation of President Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: U.S. throws out policies limiting arrests of migrants at sensitive locations like schools, churches
item.description: Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration directs all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on leave
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is moving to end affirmative action in federal contracting and is directing that all federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff be put on paid leave and eventually be laid off.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump revokes John Bolton's Secret Service detail amid Iranian death threats: former national security advisor
item.description: The former national security advisor has faced threats from Iran going back years, including an alleged assassination plot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 22 states sue to stop Trump's order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 22 states sued Tuesday to block President Donald Trump's move to end a century-old immigration practice known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Stefanik excoriates Dem for Elon Musk 'Nazi salute' accusation at UN ambassador hearing
item.description: "No, Elon Musk did not do those salutes," New York Rep. Elise Stefanik told Democratic Sen. Chris Murphy when pressed before lawmakers at her confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration ousts Linda Fagan, first female Coast Guard commandant
item.description: Adm. Kevin Lunday, the former vice commandant of the Coast Guard, will take over as acting commandant, the statement notes, offering no other details of the switch.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump defends pardons for rioters and suggests Proud Boys could have place in politics
item.description: President Donald Trump has defended his decision to pardon people convicted of assaulting police officers during the attack on the Capitol and suggests there could be a place in U.S. politics for the Proud Boys extremist group, quickly evoking some of the controversies of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis: North Florida could see several inches of snow
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis warned of potential snowfall this week, amid the freezing temperatures impacting northern Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Senate panel advances Noem nomination for Homeland Security secretary
item.description: A Senate committee cleared President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) with a bipartisan vote, paving the way for a Senate floor vote. The Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee advanced South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R) by a 13-2 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues pardons for participants in the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol
item.description: The pardons fulfilled Trump’s promise to release supporters who tried to help him overturn his election defeat four years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Marco Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Senate panel advances Defense secretary nominee Hegseth on party-line vote
item.description: The Senate Armed Services Committee on Monday voted to advance Pete Hegseth, President Trump’s nominee to serve as secretary of Defense, to the floor. The 14-13 vote was strictly along party lines.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Expert: Trump can’t override Minnesota state law with executive orders
item.description: University of Minnesota political science professor Kathryn Pearson says President Donald Trump can’t direct states in matters that are up to state law.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams will attend Donald Trump’s inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams will attend President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday in Washington, D.C. Adams added the inauguration to his schedule Monday morning. He canceled existing appearances in New York City, including two celebrations of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
